text: The song came on the radio unexpectedly and she turned it up loud and drove the rest of the way with the windows down singing along to every word she still remembered from high school
He opened the mailbox and found a handwritten letter from his grandmother, the envelope thick with photographs he had never seen of his mother as a little girl standing barefoot in a garden
When the pregnancy test showed two lines she just stood there in the bathroom holding it, and then she laughed once, loud, and sat down on the edge of the tub
The kid ran the whole length of the soccer field with the ball and somehow, impossibly, scored, and the look on his face when he turned around was something his father would carry for decades
They found a twenty-dollar bill folded up in the pocket of a winter coat they hadn't worn since last year and used it to buy two coffees and a pastry at the bakery on the corner
After six months of empty shelves the shop finally reopened and the owner was standing at the door with fresh paint on her hands and a handwritten sign that said Grand Re-Opening in crooked letters
The dog figured out the doggy door on his own for the first time and came barreling back inside so proud of himself that he knocked over a plant and nobody even cared
She finished the last page of her thesis at 2 AM, hit save, closed the laptop, and then just sat there in the dark kitchen listening to the refrigerator hum
First morning in the new apartment and sunlight came through the east window at an angle that lit up the whole living room floor, and she realized she had picked the right place
His daughter, who was four, made him a card with a drawing of what she said was a dinosaur but looked more like a potato with legs, and he put it on the fridge with the best magnet they had
The old couple at the next table in the restaurant were holding hands across the white tablecloth and she watched them lean toward each other and share a bite off the same fork
Rain started falling just as the garden was planted and she stood on the back porch with dirt under her fingernails watching the water pool around the new seedlings
He got the acceptance email while sitting in a laundromat and read it three times on the plastic chair next to a woman folding towels who had no idea what had just happened to him

The bread came out of the oven golden and cracked on top and when she tapped the bottom it sounded hollow the way the cookbook said it should and she set it on the wire rack with something close to reverence
At the airport arrivals gate her best friend came through the sliding doors with a backpack and a year's worth of stories and they grabbed each other so hard the backpack fell on the floor
He fixed the leaky faucet himself for the first time using a YouTube video and a wrench he borrowed from the neighbor, and when the dripping stopped, the silence felt like a standing ovation
The cherry blossoms were out along the canal and they walked under them slowly, not talking much, petals catching in their hair and drifting onto the surface of the water
She played the chord progression she had been stuck on for weeks and suddenly her fingers found the bridge, and it worked, it actually worked, and she played it again immediately to make sure
The waiter brought out the birthday cake with too many candles and the whole restaurant started clapping, even the strangers, and his mother covered her face with both hands and shook her head
On the hike back down the mountain the sun broke through the cloud cover and lit up the valley below them all at once, green and gold, and nobody said anything for a full minute
The cat, who had been missing for eleven days, appeared at the back door at midnight meowing like nothing had happened, and she picked him up and buried her face in his dirty fur
He parallel parked perfectly on the first try in front of the interview building and took it as a sign
The paycheck cleared and for the first time in four months there was enough to cover rent and groceries and still have something left, and she closed the banking app slowly, deliberately
They turned the corner on their bikes and the whole downhill stretch opened up ahead, no cars, late summer light, the road curving through cornfields all the way to the river
His son called from college just to talk, not because he needed money or had a problem, just because he wanted to tell him about a book he was reading about black holes
The quilt her mother started before she died was finally finished by her aunt, who mailed it wrapped in tissue paper, and when she unfolded it on the bed the colors were exactly right
Everyone at the potluck tried her dish first and two people asked for the recipe and someone said it was the best thing on the table while reaching for a second serving
The test results came back clean and the doctor said see you in a year and she walked out into the parking lot where everything, the asphalt the sky the minivan, looked sharper than before
Wading into the ocean on the first beach day of the year, the cold water shocking their ankles, then their knees, then all at once they went under and came up gasping and grinning
He opened the box from the online order expecting the usual disappointment but the boots fit perfectly, like they had been made for his feet, and he wore them around the apartment for an hour
The power came back on after three days of the ice storm and the furnace clicked and hummed and warm air started pouring from the vents and the children cheered from under their blankets
She solved the crossword puzzle on the train without looking anything up, folded the newspaper, and tucked it into her bag with the satisfied precision of someone closing a finished book
The baby grabbed his finger for the first time and squeezed, surprisingly strong, and stared up at him with enormous dark eyes that didn't blink
They pulled into the driveway of the cabin they had rented sight-unseen and it was better than the photos, with a porch swing and a view of the lake through the pine trees
Her painting sold at the community art show and the buyer, a woman she had never met, said it reminded her of somewhere she used to live as a child
The old truck started on the first try after sitting in the barn all winter, engine turning over with a cough and then a roar, and he patted the dashboard like it was alive
At the reunion she recognized him immediately even though it had been twenty years, and he recognized her too, and they just stood there shaking their heads and laughing at the parking lot
The snow was the perfect kind, wet enough to pack, and the whole neighborhood was outside, kids and adults, building things in the yards while the streetlights turned on one by one
She opened the door to the surprise party and saw every person she loved standing in one room and for a second she forgot to breathe and then she forgot how to stop smiling
The tomatoes in the garden finally turned red all at once after weeks of waiting and he picked the first one still warm from the sun and ate it right there standing between the rows
